regime_hint.notes: NIFTY closed 23,689.6 (+1.18%) — recovery day after Mon-Tue -3.3% drawdown. SGX Nifty +0.49% (~23,845) implies mild gap-up. US ATH overnight: SPX +0.78% (7,502), NDQ +0.88% (26,635) — AI/semis-led, broad risk-on. USD/INR 95.77 weak (IT exporter tailwind). Crude Brent $107.05 +1.26% (bearish India macro; RBI Gov flagged fuel-price hike risk if Middle East tension persists). NIFTY ATR 1.49%, vol regime LOW. VIX API null — treat as NORMAL given orderly recovery tape. No major India earnings disclosed in macro brief; SAIL Q4 board meet today (binary), SBIN AGM today (binary), BPCL trading window closed (Q4 May 19). Daily regime RANGING; tape carries upside drift but with macro headwinds — no directional bias, treat 09:45 VWAP as bias-flip trigger.

watchlist.notes: Top-3 scanner score (2.74). Q4 announced last week, no event today, AGM Jul 7. Mixed analyst views (GS Sell vs Elara Buy 504). Tradeable both sides on 2+ confirmation. Watch wide-ORB threshold (>2% auto-exclude).
Sole bank in plan. Resilient close (+0.50% May 14), independent legal review found no governance issues. No event today. Banking-cluster volatility low post-SBIN AGM. Watch VWAP closely.
IT tailwind: US NDQ ATH +0.88%, USD/INR 95.77 weak. No fresh news. Scanner score 2.42. Tradeable both — IT sector saw 3-day decline into May 14 so reversal is possible.
Positive AI catalyst: Red Hat collaboration (May 14) + Google Cloud Agentic AI expansion. No event today. IT sector tailwind from NDQ ATH.
IT sector positive macro but 3-day decline into May 14 on AI-substitution concerns. No event today. Lower conviction than INFY/HCLTECH given sector overhang.
Q4 announced May 13-14, positive market reaction (net profit Rs 1,256 cr, FY27 pre-sales target Rs 20k cr). No event today. Scanner score 2.40.
Downgrade to Sell May 11-12 (weak Q4 PAT -18.4%) is a structural overhang. No event today. Keep both bias with low conviction per W19 lesson — let intraday tape decide direction.
Defensive FMCG hedge. No events. Useful directional-uncertainty diversifier given crude-driven inflation risk.

rationale: 8-symbol plan, all `both` bias per #lesson 2026-W19 (default-both unless robust directional catalyst). Tilted toward IT (3 names: INFY, HCLTECH, TCS) to capture NDQ ATH + weak rupee tailwind. One bank (HDFCBANK), one power (TATAPOWER), one realty (DLF), one defensive (HINDUNILVR), one energy with downside lean (RELIANCE low conviction). Hard avoids: SAIL (Q4 board meet today), SBIN (AGM today), BPCL (trading window closed until May 21), ADANIENT (no fresh news + W18/W19 re-entry history), PNB/ETERNAL (outside NIFTY 50 universe). Risk tightened to 1.0% per trade / max 2 open positions given (a) yesterday's -Rs 632 loss + 5-trade 20% win rate, (b) rising crude + RBI fuel-price hike warning, (c) two-week paper drawdown of ~2.6%. MAX_TRADES_PER_DAY stays at settings default (not overridable). Expect lunch-lull 12:00-13:30 — best setups before 11:30 or after 13:30. If NIFTY fades the SGX gap-up by 09:45 (within 0.3% of prior close), demote regime read to RANGING-flat and require strict 2+ confirmation everywhere.
lessons_applied: #lesson 2026-W19 (promoted): Default to `bias: both` unless catalyst is robust to intraday price action — all 8 names set to both.
#lesson 2026-W19 (promoted): On big-bank-print days keep at most one bank in the watchlist — only HDFCBANK included (SBIN AGM today excluded).
#lesson 2026-05-08: Bias-invalidation conditions not enforced in code — using `both` defensively rather than directional bias I cannot reliably invalidate intraday.
#lesson 2026-04-30: Binary-event stocks must be excluded entirely, not just flagged — SAIL (Q4 board meet today), SBIN (AGM today), BPCL (trading window closed) all hard-excluded.
#lesson 2026-05-05: Scanner can pick stocks outside the defined NIFTY 50 universe — PNB and ETERNAL excluded despite appearing in top 15.
#lesson 2026-05-04: Wide-ORB (>2%) stocks must auto-exclude — TATAPOWER notes flag this explicitly given post-Q4 volatility tail.
#lesson 2026-05-07: SGX gap framing can fade — rationale includes 09:45 VWAP recheck.
#lesson 2026-W18 (promoted): R:R filter is the strongest discipline guardrail — risk tightened (1.0%/2 open) so the filter can do its job on a fresh-drawdown day.
